prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a nearly black screen with archive watermarks visible: a circular “Classic Commercial Archives” logo at lower left and a faint “@ClassicCommercialArchives” watermark across the lower middle.

It then cuts to a close-up portrait of a smiling adult man with short light-brown/gray hair, facing the camera. He wears a collared shirt, and the image has strong color distortion, with green and pink tones across his face and hair. The background is plain and light-colored.

Next, the scene changes to a close view of a hand pulling open or reaching into a horizontal drawer or shelf unit. The unit appears to have broad horizontal divisions, suggesting a display or storage drawer. The hand is at the upper left, gripping or moving the front edge. The setting is brightly lit but the image remains soft and color-shifted.

The following shot shows clothing laid out in compartments or shelves. Several folded collared shirts are visible from above, including light-colored shirts and a more saturated orange/yellow shirt in the center. Part of a person’s shoulder or head appears at the lower right edge, but the person is mostly out of frame and unclear.

Later, a clearer overhead product shot shows three folded collared shirts displayed side by side in an open shelf or drawer. From left to right, the shirts appear beige or off-white, bright orange/red, and pale yellow. Each shirt has a collar and a small label near the neck area. The shirts are neatly folded and arranged as merchandise.

The final frame is a graphic product-and-price screen on a dark background. On the left is a framed portrait of the same smiling man wearing a blue collared shirt and holding the collar area with both hands. On the right, large white text reads: “The Fox Only $13.” Along the bottom, larger white text reads: “Only at JCPenney,” though part of the text is partially obscured by the archive watermark.
